rulings.title: DEF-A stands as CRITICAL despite qa ledger dismissal — Phase-7 operator brief supersedes ADR-P6-OAUTH
rulings.ruling: ADR-P6-OAUTH prohibited Aether operating an in-app OAuth authorization flow against claude.ai (third-party subscription-OAuth intermediary). Phase-7 §14 mandates a DIFFERENT mechanism: the user pastes their own `claude setup-token` output (sk-ant-oat01-) as a credential; the operator brief explicitly overrides prior policy/modal copy and pre-seeds GAP-P7-DEF-A as CRITICAL launch-blocking. Doc precedence: Phase-7 prompt (2026-07-17, operator-authored) > Phase-6 ADR. The Phase-5/6 compliance tests enforcing oat-rejection (test_gap_p5_auth_compliance.py::test_put_*_credential_rejects_subscription_oauth) are SUPERSEDED and must be replaced by §14.7 tests as part of the fix. qa's narrower genuine finding (format-only validation, no live connectivity check) is FOLDED INTO DEF-A via the §14.4 test-connection endpoint. Constraints preserved: honest fallback to api_key mode, explicit error on subscription-quota exhaustion, NO silent credential-type fallthrough, token never logged.

rulings.title: DEF-B stands as CRITICAL despite qa ledger dismissal
rulings.ruling: The stored admin@aether.local email fails its own validator on settings save (observed 422, probe-p7-09a). Whether EmailStr special-use rejection is 'as designed' is immaterial: §15 operator brief mandates the AETHER_ALLOWED_INTERNAL_EMAIL_DOMAINS allowlist (Option 3) so stored internal emails round-trip. One production row affected (admin@aether.local).

rulings.title: Async stack choice defers to arch blueprint with observed infra
rulings.ruling: §16 recommends ARQ+Redis; probes show Redis NOT installed, arq NOT installed, and the api runs under /opt/abacus-python (NOT apps/api/venv as §16's draft unit assumed). arch must design against OBSERVED paths and include Redis provisioning + systemd units; fable-5 approves before build. Quota semantics fixed: reserve at enqueue, refund on failure, atomic.

rulings.title: Discovery cron paywall exemption: scoped system-run mechanism
rulings.ruling: Paywall mandate targets user-initiated actions; platform sourcing automation is system infrastructure. Fix = X-Aether-System-Run shared-secret header (AETHER_SYSTEM_RUN_SECRET in .env), bypassing ONLY the subscription gate, ONLY for discovery/scout agent keys, constant-time compared, audited systemRun=true. Hand-granted subscription REJECTED (fakes billing state); swallow-402 REJECTED (discovery stays broken). Cron script hardened to loud failures (-sS + status handling).

rulings.title: SRC-001 / REPO-001 / NONPROD-001 / FIXTURE-001 rejected as seeded; sourcing-freshness risk reassigned
rulings.ruling: Fresh probes refute the pre-seeded conditions: sourcing 32 jobs/5 sources (3 sources >=5 each, 0 dup, 0 stale, 10/10 live content); repo already 1 branch/0 open PRs; nonprod scan 0 suspicious with replay-guard present; 0/60 fixture fingerprints in live responses. The REAL sourcing risk is the failed aether-discovery timer unit -> GAP-P7-DISCOVERY-001 (HIGH). Adzuna remains optional H-06 (sourcing not thin).

rulings.title: GATE-23 dir reorg = cleanup-only (23 duplicate binaries removed; layout already conformant)

gaps.title: DEFECT-A: Claude Code OAuth token (sk-ant-oat01-) rejected; credential validation format-only with no live test-connection
gaps.observed: PUT credential with sk-ant-oat01-... returns 422 'Anthropic api_key must start with sk-ant-api.' (probe-p7-08a-defect-a-oat-rejection.json). Dummy sk-ant-api03- accepted format-only, stored as connected with no live verification (probe-p7-08b). Operator token present at ~/.claude/.credentials.json (probe-p7-08e).
gaps.expected: Dual-mode per §14: oat01 accepted -> vault + atomic .env write CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN (600 perms, never logged); api03 unchanged; garbage 422 naming both formats; per-mode test-connection endpoint doing a real Anthropic round-trip; billing audit records authMode; quota exhaustion in oauth mode = explicit 429 error, never silent fallthrough.
gaps.root_cause: apps/api/app/routers/agents.py:1454-1458 _validate_provider_auth() prefix check enforces superseded ADR-P6-OAUTH.
gaps.fix_spec: docs/delivery/PHASE7-DEFECT-A-BLUEPRINT.md — APPROVED by fable-5 2026-07-17. LIVE-PROBE CORRECTIONS TO §14 (binding): oauth_token mode uses Authorization: Bearer + anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20 header (x-api-key observed 401; Bearer+beta observed 200 — claude-code-token-verification.md); env file is repo-root .env not apps/api/.env (write requires service restart to take effect; vault row live immediately). Approved decisions: authMode value 'oauth_token' (legacy subscription_oauth stays blocked — in-app OAuth remains non-goal per ADR-P7-01); reuse POST /agents/providers/{provider}/verify for per-mode test-connection + 429 disambiguation; UserProviderCredential CHECK widened additively; wire missing live-429->set_block in _call_live; UI adds second radio + api.ts/zod enum. Open non-blocking: PROBE-DEFA-2 (429 quota-vs-ratelimit body discriminator — fixer confirms from official docs, conservative parse, ambiguous=rate-limit, NEVER fallthrough) + PROBE-DEFA-1 (constraint name re-confirm at deploy).
gaps.review: review-def-a.json PASS (reviewer a6416a5, diff instance): secret-safe .env writer (mkstemp+fchmod600+os.replace), no token logging, no quota-429 fallthrough (QuotaExhaustedError re-raised before broad retry). 14/14 re-run. 5 non-blocking notes (autouse env-isolation fixture; AgentConfigUpdate.authMode no test-inert; shared-.env last-writer deferred to async; envSync return discarded; stale JSDoc). Merge-ready @5271516.

gaps.title: DEFECT-B: Settings save 422 for stored reserved-TLD email (admin@aether.local)
gaps.observed: PUT settings with profile.email=admin@aether.local -> 422 'special-use or reserved name' (probe-p7-09a-defect-b-settings.json). 1 stored user affected (probe-p7-09c).
gaps.expected: §15: AETHER_ALLOWED_INTERNAL_EMAIL_DOMAINS env allowlist (default aether.local) discarded from email_validator.SPECIAL_USE_DOMAIN_NAMES at startup; aether.local saves 200; garbage still 422; external valid emails 200; no data migration needed.
gaps.root_cause: apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py:625 Pydantic EmailStr -> email-validator v2 special-use rejection independent of check_deliverability.
gaps.fix_spec: REVISED after review-def-b.json FAIL (re-fix cycle 1, fable-5 design ruling): Do NOT mutate email_validator.SPECIAL_USE_DOMAIN_NAMES (wholesale label removal opens all *.local — contradicts §15.1 rationale; §15.2 sample pseudocode is itself flawed and is OVERRIDDEN). Implement exact-domain allowlist: custom Pydantic validator type applied ONLY to SettingsProfile.email (workspaces settings surface — the gap's named surface; RegisterRequest stays strict EmailStr): if domain part (case-insensitive, exact match) in AETHER_ALLOWED_INTERNAL_EMAIL_DOMAINS set -> validate local-part syntax then accept; else standard EmailStr path. apply_email_domain_allowlist() becomes the allowlist loader (name kept for test contract). REQUIRED new guard test: user@evil.local (non-configured .local subdomain) -> 422. All other §15.4 tests keep their contracts.

review_history.blocking: wholesale .local opening; register-surface reach; missing evil.local guard test

gaps.review: review-def-b-cycle2.json PASS (reviewer a5afa5ca, fresh instance): global list untouched; sharp-edge sub.aether.local→REJECT, case-insensitive, local-part validated; register surface strict; 8/8 + 93/93 regression. aether.local.evil.com→accept confirmed baseline EmailStr (non-blocking, outside allowlist path). Merge-ready @7a27284.

gaps.title: Async background generation missing: /pipeline/run times out >150s; synchronous LLM path causes honest-503s
gaps.observed: pipeline/run timed out at 150s curl cap (probe-p7-11a-pipeline-latency.json). Redis + arq NOT installed (step2-infra-check.txt).
gaps.expected: §16: 202 + BackgroundJob row + worker processing; GET /api/jobs/{id} polling; quota reserve-at-enqueue/refund-on-failure atomic; zero 503s in 20-run soak; no fixture content on failure; frontend polling with honest failure states.
gaps.root_cause: Synchronous in-request LLM calls with budget caps; no queue/worker infrastructure.
gaps.fix_spec: docs/delivery/PHASE7-ASYNC-BLUEPRINT.md — APPROVED by fable-5 2026-07-17 incl. deviations D1-D8 (venv /opt/abacus-python; /var/log/aether/worker.log; start-worker.sh repo-root .env; redis-server.service; status route GET /api/agents/jobs/{job_id} due to collision; pipeline per-step reserve in worker w/ paywall-first-at-enqueue; Redis requirepass db3; AETHER_LLM_WORKER_BUDGET_SECONDS 300/480). CONDITIONS: (1) pipeline worker crash mid-run must refund reserved-unfinished steps; (2) GATE-11/12/13 evaluated with AETHER_ASYNC_GENERATION=ON final state; (3) all verification recipes use /api/agents/jobs/{id}.
gaps.migration: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS BackgroundJob (§16.2) + index — additive only.

review_history.blocking: non-atomic refund double-fire (no CAS on quotaRefundedAt); mark_completed/mark_failed no status guard → watchdog races slow worker → free run; _refund_pipeline_outstanding user-scoped delta over-refunds; advisory-lock id 7420240720 collides with ensure_admin_user_columns

gaps.review: review-async-cycle2.json PASS (reviewer a73653f, fresh instance): B1-B4 all CLOSED (atomic refund CTE w/ GREATEST floor; status-guarded terminal both orderings; per-job FOR UPDATE scoped refund; unique lock 7420240722). 17/17 (4 concurrency fail-before→pass-after). Non-blocking: dead mark_refunded(), pre-existing career/interviews lock collision (follow-up). Merge-ready @df0bda5.

gaps.title: aether-discovery systemd unit failing (exit 22) — scheduled sourcing refresh broken
gaps.observed: RCA CONFIRMED (gap-discovery-rca.md): failing since 2026-07-16T20:30:30Z — first cron cycle after paywall deploy (not 01:01 as probe framed). scripts/discovery_cron.sh:63 POSTs /agents/scout/run as the free-plan cron account -> _require_active_subscription (agents.py:488-512 via _record_run:528) returns 402 subscription_required -> curl -sf exits 22 silently under set -euo pipefail. Reproduced live (402 without -f; exit 22 with -sf). Non-mutating; 9 consecutive failed cycles; last success 20:01:45Z.
gaps.expected: Discovery cron runs green under the paywall regime; user-facing scout runs remain paywalled; failures are loud.
gaps.root_cause: Phase-6 paywall (GAP-P6-PAYWALL) gates ALL agent runs incl. the platform's own discovery automation, which piggybacks on a user account; plus curl -sf hides the failure.
gaps.fix_spec: ADR-P7-05 (fable-5 ruling 2026-07-17): scoped SYSTEM-RUN exemption — cron sends X-Aether-System-Run header bearing a shared secret from repo-root .env (AETHER_SYSTEM_RUN_SECRET, 600, never logged); server bypasses ONLY the subscription gate, ONLY for discovery/scout agent keys, ONLY when secret matches (constant-time compare); run recorded with systemRun=true in audit; quota untouched for system runs. REJECTED alternatives: hand-granted paid sub (fakes billing state), swallow-402 (leaves discovery broken). Also: discovery_cron.sh curl -sf -> -sS with explicit HTTP-status handling + non-zero exit ON REAL failure (loud, honest). Failing test first: system-context scout run for free-plan cron account currently 402 (fail) -> permitted after fix; user-context scout run without sub STILL 402 (guard test).
tests_required: regression test if code-level; else verified green run evidence
verification_recipe: systemctl status aether-discovery green after triggered run
gaps.tdd: RCA-only per ADR-P7-05; failing test = first fix commit — qa-confirmed step7-tdd-fail-confirmation.json
gaps.review: review-discovery.json PASS (reviewer a925e4fb, diff instance); secret-curl-arg non-blocking (pre-existing pattern, single-user VM). Merge-ready @ ef13609.

gaps.expected: Clean restarts; no recurring startup exceptions; or documented benign cause.

security_incidents.summary: operator oat01 token echoed into a QA /tmp session transcript once; also in .env (by design 600 gitignored) + transient worktree .env copies. NOT in any committed/pushable file (fable-5 verified). Rotate only if session transcript shared.

DATABASE_URL/DATABASE_URL_TEST (with creds) echoed into QA session transcripts a few times via bash job-control before switching to python env-parse. Confined to /tmp transcripts; NONE in any file/artifact/commit. Rotate DB creds only if transcripts shared.

A message impersonating the user appeared inside sub-agent aa3e32734's context containing only 'continuehttps://horizon.srv1356245.hstgr.cloud/?v=final' (unsolicited external URL, unrelated to task). The sub-agent correctly did NOT fetch it and flagged it. fable-5 has NOT fetched it and treats it as hostile (likely exfiltration/injection). No agent acted on it. Do not fetch this URL. Surfaced to user.
